com.mentor.chs.plugin.action

Interface IXBaseAction

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXPlugin
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXAction, IXApplicationAction, IXFunctionDesignAction, IXHarnessAction, IXIntegratorAction, IXLogicAction, IXPlatformArchitectureDesignAction, IXSBOMAction, IXSymbolAction, IXSymbolApplicationAction, IXTopologyAction

    @NotToBeImplementedDirectly
public interface IXBaseAction
extends IXPlugin
    Parent interface for all custom actions in Design and Symbol tools. This interface should not be used directly. If you have developed a Custom Action that can be used with more than one tools/design type, all you need to do is implement all the interfaces concerned.

    The name used on the menu entry will be the string returned by the IXPlugin.getName() method.

    NOT TO BE USED DIRECTLY.

    See Also:
    IXAction, IXSymbolAction

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getLongDescription()
      Get a more detailed description of the action.
      java.lang.Integer getMnemonicKey()
      Get the mnemonic key that is associated with this action.
      javax.swing.Icon getSmallIcon()
      Get the small icon that is associated with this action.
      IXAction.Trigger[] getTriggers()
      Get the places where this action should be triggered.
      boolean isReadOnly()
      Is this a read-only IXAction? If you do not intend on changing any attribute/properties on any objects, you must return true.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • getTriggers

        IXAction.Trigger[] getTriggers()
        Get the places where this action should be triggered.

        This information is used on the initialization of the action to place the action in the correct place. It cannot be used to dynamically.

        This must return an array of Trigger objects that indicate the menus that this custom action can be used with.

        If the custom action returns an array with { MainMenu } entries, then you will only see the custom action menu on the main menu and not the context menu.

        If the custom action returns an array with { MainMenu, ContextMenu } entries, then you will see the custom action menu on the main menu and the context menu.

        If the custom action returns an array with {InvokeFromPlugin} entries, then the custom action will be invoked from another plug-in programmatically, and is not seen in either the main menu or the context menu.

        An error will be raised if the array is null or empty and the custom action will not be available.

        Returns:
        the trigger points. This method should never return NULL or an empty array.

      • getSmallIcon

        javax.swing.Icon getSmallIcon()
        Get the small icon that is associated with this action. This icon will be displayed on the menu entry for the action.

        This information is used on the initialization of the action. It cannot be used to dynamically.

        This method can return either null (if no icon for the action is required), or an icon object.

      • getMnemonicKey

        java.lang.Integer getMnemonicKey()
        Get the mnemonic key that is associated with this action.

        This information is used on the initialization of the action. It cannot be used to dynamically.

        This method can return either null, or an Integer object that represents the key to be used. For Example: 
             Integer getMnemonicKey() {
         return (int) 'c';
     }
        If not null, a check will be made to ensure that the key is present in the name of the plugin and that no other custom action uses that mnemonic for that application. If these checks fail, a warning will be issued in the plugin Tab of the Output Window and the mnemonic will not be used by the custom action, but the custom action will still be available.
        Returns:
        the mnemonic, null if no mnemonic is required.

      • getLongDescription

        java.lang.String getLongDescription()
        Get a more detailed description of the action.

        This long description will appear at the bottom of the Capital Logic, Capital Integrator, Capital HarnessXC and Captial Design applications whilst the action is running.

        Returns:
        the long description of the custom action.

      • isReadOnly

        boolean isReadOnly()
        Is this a read-only IXAction? If you do not intend on changing any attribute/properties on any objects, you must return true. This will ensure that if a read-only design is loaded, your action will still be available. Moreover, your action will NOT be provided with an IXAttributeSetter (the object used to change attribute/properties). If your action could change attribute/properties on any objects, you should return false. This will ensure that your action will only be available if the design you are working on has been locked by you. Moreover, your action will be provided with IXAttributeSetter (the object used to change attribute/properties).
        See Also:
        IXWriteableObject, IXAttributeSetter
